title: LSL Core — Learner State Layer
description: Validates the Core layer of an LSL document (SPEC.md §3). Both serializations (YAML and JSON) use the same snake_case keys, so a YAML document validates identically after parsing. Extension fields (Provenance, Relationships, Federation) are permitted and ignored per the additive-extensibility principle (§2.8): unknown fields are never errors. One Provenance-compatibility rule lives here by necessity: `can`/`cannot_yet`/`misconceptions` items may be either bare strings (Core) or objects with `text` + optional `evidence` (Provenance) — consumers MUST accept both (§4.1).

learner.description: Stable identifier for the learner. SHOULD be pseudonymous and per-scope (§3.2). REQUIRED.

updated.description: Date or datetime of last compilation. REQUIRED.

subject.description: Human-readable name of this document's scope. OPTIONAL.

goals.description: Learner-declared intents — the only learner-authored section (§3.5). OPTIONAL.

concepts.description: One entry per concept the learner has meaningful state about (§3.3). REQUIRED.

teaches_best.description: Derived learning preferences — outcome-backed only, never self-reported styles (§3.6). OPTIONAL.

claimItem.description: A capability/gap/misconception item: a bare sentence (Core) or an object with text and optional evidence links (Provenance, §4.1). Consumers MUST treat the string form as equivalent to an object with only `text`.

evidence.description: xAPI statement IDs or internal log URIs backing this claim.

id.description: Concept IRI, minted on the issuer's own domain or drawn from an external framework (CASE, CTDL). OPTIONAL but recommended. Unresolvable IRIs degrade to opaque stable keys. The pattern only enforces a leading URI scheme; issuers are trusted to mint sensible HTTP(S) IRIs.

mastery.description: 0.0–1.0 estimate of current ability to use the concept. An anchor for the bands in §3.4, not a precise measurement; not cross-platform comparable at Core level. REQUIRED.

last_seen.description: Date of most recent supporting evidence. Freshness is load-bearing (§9.4). REQUIRED.

trend.description: Direction of the estimate over the recent evidence window. OPTIONAL.

can.description: Demonstrated capabilities — evidence-backed, never aspirations or self-reports (§3.3, §9.3). REQUIRED; may be empty.

cannot_yet.description: Observed gaps — attempted and failed, or assessed as absent. Not merely untried (§3.3, §9.1). REQUIRED; may be empty.

misconceptions.description: Active false beliefs, stated as the belief ('Believes X'). REQUIRED; may be empty.

target.description: Concept or subject id this goal aims at. OPTIONAL.

learn.description: Concepts whose prerequisites are met, not yet started.

review.description: Known concepts predicted to have decayed.

blocked.description: Desired concepts with missing prerequisites, mapped to the reason.
